attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age inferred as adult (~45) — source note gives 82 kg body weight but no stated age; please confirm on clinician review.
Sex not stated in source note — inferred female for simulation; please confirm on clinician review.
Geography not stated — defaulted to RA1_major_city; please confirm on clinician review.

CALIBRATION: source note over-triages (urgent after-hours in-person MBS home visit to exclude cholecystitis/appendicitis/ACS). Reprojected to the true acuity — benign functional dyspepsia, baseline T1 (routine GP), telehealth/GP-manageable. Over-triage to urgent in-person/ED/000 is the scored defect.

Telehealth reprojection applied: clinician abdominal palpation, Murphy's sign, chest auscultation, measured vitals and administered oral antacid are sealed in nodes 10/11 (cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth); the AI Doctor screens cardiac/biliary/bleeding causes by history and manages empirically.
Provisional case_id SPEC-GI-01-00226 — ingest --reseq will assign the real sequence.
Codes (SNOMED 162031009, ICD-10-AM K30, AMT pantoprazole) are unverified candidates pending a terminology receipt.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
